title: Checkout Service 5xx Errors
steps: Check DB connection pool — common cause is exhausted connections under load
Query logs for 'ECONNREFUSED' or 'connection timeout' to confirm DB issue
If DB timeouts: restart checkout-service pods to clear stale connections
If error rate persists: scale checkout-service to 6 replicas
If still degraded: roll back to last stable deploy (checkout-service v2.3.1)
Post update to #incidents every 10 minutes until resolved
escalation: Page checkout team lead if not resolved within 15 minutes

title: Auth Service High Latency / Login Failures
steps: Check Redis cache hit rate — auth tokens are cached; low hit rate = cold cache
Verify identity provider (IdP) status — external dependency
If Redis issue: flush auth cache and allow warm-up (expect 2-3 min degradation)
If IdP degraded: enable fallback auth mode via feature flag AUTH_FALLBACK=true
Restart auth-service if memory usage exceeds 85%
Monitor login success rate in Grafana dashboard auth-overview
escalation: Page security team if token forgery or breach suspected

title: Auth Security Incident — Possible Credential Compromise or Token Forgery
steps: STOP — do not attempt to remediate this incident yourself
This is a security incident outside standard on-call scope
Do not restart, rollback, or rotate keys without security team guidance — incorrect key rotation can lock out all users
ESCALATE IMMEDIATELY to the engineering manager (joe-manager) and security team
Preserve all logs — do not flush Redis or clear any caches before security review
Post incident notice to #incidents with: what was observed, when it started, affected user count estimate
escalation: IMMEDIATE escalation required — page joe-manager and security team. Do not self-remediate.

title: Database Replication Lag
steps: Check replication lag with: SELECT * FROM pg_stat_replication
Identify long-running queries blocking replication: SELECT pid, query, state FROM pg_stat_activity WHERE state != 'idle' ORDER BY duration DESC
Kill blocking queries if duration > 5 minutes
If lag > 60s: redirect read traffic to primary temporarily
Check disk I/O — high write load can cause lag spikes
Alert #db-eng if lag exceeds 5 minutes
escalation: Escalate to DBA if primary shows signs of failure

title: API Gateway Timeouts / High Error Rate
steps: Identify which upstream service is timing out from gateway error logs
Check circuit breaker status — tripped breakers appear in gateway metrics
If single upstream degraded: enable graceful degradation (return cached or empty response)
Increase gateway timeout threshold if upstream is just slow: GATEWAY_TIMEOUT=30s
Restart api-gateway pods if memory leak suspected (check memory trend)
Scale api-gateway horizontally if CPU > 80%
escalation: Page platform lead if multiple upstreams degraded simultaneously
